heading: Income taxes of members of Armed Forces, astronauts, and victims of certain terrorist attacks on death
label: 692 Income taxes of members of Armed Forces, astronauts, and victims of certain terrorist attacks on death

Refunds and Credits of Overpayments for Taxable Years Ending on or After , Resulting From Application of ProvisionsFebruary 28, 1961, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 93–597, § 4(c)88 Stat. 1952Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Jan. 2, 1975Oct. 22, 1986“If the refund or credit of any overpayment for any taxable year ending on or after , resulting from the application of section 692 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as amended by subsection (a) of this section) is prevented at any time before the expiration of one year after the date of the enactment of this Act [] by the operation of any law or rule of law, but would not have been so prevented if claim for refund or credit therefor were made on the due date for the return for the taxable year of his death (or any later year), refund or credit of such overpayment may, nevertheless, be made or allowed if claim therefor is filed before the expiration of such one-year period.”February 28, 1961Jan. 2, 1975

Treatment of Director General of Multinational Force in Sinai, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 722(g)(4)98 Stat. 974Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“For purposes of section 692(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], the Director General of the Multinational Force and Observers in the Sinai who died on , shall be treated as if he were a civilian employee of the United States while he served as such Director General.”February 15, 1984

children.text: any tax imposed by this subtitle shall not apply with respect to the taxable year in which falls the date of his death, or with respect to any prior taxable year ending on or after the first day he so served in a combat zone; and

any tax under this subtitle and under the corresponding provisions of prior revenue laws for taxable years preceding those specified in paragraph (1) which is unpaid at the date of his death (including interest, additions to the tax, and additional amounts) shall not be assessed, and if assessed the assessment shall be abated, and if collected shall be credited or refunded as an overpayment.

children.heading: Individuals in missing status
children.label: b Individuals in missing status
children.text: For purposes of this section, in the case of an individual who was in a missing status within the meaning of section 6013(f)(3)(A), the date of his death shall be treated as being not earlier than the date on which a determination of his death is made under of the United States Code. Except in the case of the combat zone designated for purposes of the Vietnam conflict, the preceding sentence shall not cause subsection (a)(1) to apply for any taxable year beginning more than 2 years after the date designated under section 112 as the date of termination of combatant activities in a combat zone.section 556 of title 37

children.heading: Certain military or civilian employees of the United States dying as a result of injuries
children.label: c Certain military or civilian employees of the United States dying as a result of injuries
A
children.text: with respect to the taxable year in which falls the date of his death, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to any prior taxable year in the period beginning with the last taxable year ending before the taxable year in which the wounds or injury were incurred.

children.label: A
children.text: any terroristic activity which a preponderance of the evidence indicates was directed against the United States or any of its allies, and

children.label: B
children.text: any military action involving the Armed Forces of the United States and resulting from violence or aggression against the United States or any of its allies (or threat thereof).

children.heading: Treatment of multinational forces
children.label: 3 Treatment of multinational forces
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2), any multinational force in which the United States is participating shall be treated as an ally of the United States.

children.heading: Individuals dying as a result of certain attacks
children.label: d Individuals dying as a result of certain attacks
A
children.text: with respect to the taxable year in which falls the date of death, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to any prior taxable year in the period beginning with the last taxable year ending before the taxable year in which the wounds, injury, or illness referred to in paragraph (3) were incurred.

children.heading: $10,000 minimum benefit
children.label: 2 $10,000 minimum benefit
children.text: If, but for this paragraph, the amount of tax not imposed by paragraph (1) with respect to a specified terrorist victim is less than $10,000, then such victim shall be treated as having made a payment against the tax imposed by this chapter for such victim’s last taxable year in an amount equal to the excess of $10,000 over the amount of tax not so imposed.

children.label: A
children.text: deferred compensation which would have been payable after death if the individual had died other than as a specified terrorist victim, or

children.label: B
children.text: amounts payable in the taxable year which would not have been payable in such taxable year but for an action taken after .September 11, 2001

children.label: A
children.text: who dies as a result of wounds or injury incurred as a result of the terrorist attacks against the United States on , or , orApril 19, 1995September 11, 2001

children.label: B
children.text: who dies as a result of illness incurred as a result of an attack involving anthrax occurring on or after , and before .September 11, 2001January 1, 2002

children.heading: Relief with respect to astronauts
children.label: 5 Relief with respect to astronauts
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall apply to any astronaut whose death occurs in the line of duty, except that paragraph (3)(B) shall be applied by using the date of the death of the astronaut rather than .September 11, 2001
